incidents.industry: Multi-sector (universities, law firms, defense contractors, healthcare research, think tanks)
incidents.vector: Chinese state contractor exploited Microsoft Exchange Server zero-day vulnerabilities to steal COVID-19 vaccine research and US policy data; the accused operator was extradited from Italy to face US charges.
incidents.impact: DOJ accuses Xu Zewei, working under China's Shanghai State Security Bureau, of helping compromise more than 12,700 US organizations during the pandemic; he faces up to 62 years in prison after first US court appearance in Houston.

incidents.vector: Attackers flood targeted inboxes with spam, then impersonate IT support over Microsoft Teams to lure victims into installing a malicious browser extension and follow-on malware.
incidents.impact: Malicious browser extension steals credentials and provides persistent backdoor access to corporate accounts and internal systems; campaign uses an Edge-only login overlay and intentionally rejects the first password attempts to harvest credentials twice.

incidents.vector: Phishing campaign delivered via the Signal messaging app targeted top German decision-makers.
incidents.impact: Affected officials were notified and the data leak was stopped; Berlin attributes the operation to Russia and cannot rule out additional compromises.

incidents.vector: Voice-phishing and IT-support impersonation steal corporate single-sign-on credentials; attackers then move into executive accounts and harvest data from SaaS, Salesforce, SharePoint, and internal repositories.
incidents.impact: Seven-figure ransom demands; victim data published on a dedicated leak site when ransoms are refused; some operators have swatted executives to increase pressure. Activity has been ongoing since February.

incidents.vector: Russian disinformation operation uses fabricated videos, fake whistleblower personas, AI-manipulated audio/visuals, and a network of paid blue-check influencer accounts to seed false stories about Western politicians, primarily on X.
incidents.impact: Bloomberg identified more than 190 fabricated stories since August 2023 generating hundreds of millions of views; over 40% targeted Ukraine and roughly one-third targeted foreign elections.

incidents.vector: China-linked network used 90 Facebook accounts and 13 Instagram profiles, plus AI-generated imagery, to push criticism of Tibetan leadership and cast doubt on the legitimacy of the April 27 vote.
incidents.impact: Disinformation campaign attracted virtually no organic engagement and had little measurable impact on the parliamentary election held in 27 countries.

incidents.victim: Global edge devices (SOHO routers, IoT, NAS, firewalls)
incidents.vector: Chinese-nexus actors are operating large-scale botnets ('covert networks') of compromised SOHO routers and IoT devices to anonymise reconnaissance, malware delivery, command-and-control, and data exfiltration against targets globally.
incidents.impact: Joint advisory from NCSC-UK, ASD/ACSC, CISA, FBI, NSA, and 14 other agencies warns the technique creates 'IOC extinction' — indicators of compromise vanish as quickly as they are discovered — and supports pre-positioning on critical national infrastructure.

incidents.victim: Cisco Firepower / Secure Firewall / ASA customers (incl. US Federal Civilian Executive Branch)
incidents.vector: APT actor exploited Cisco ASA zero-days CVE-2025-20333 and CVE-2025-20362 to deploy the FIRESTARTER backdoor on Firepower and Secure Firewall devices, with a transient persistence mechanism that survives firmware patching.
incidents.impact: Backdoor enables remote code execution and persistent access on FCEB agency firewalls; CISA issued updated Emergency Directive 25-03 requiring identification, forensic collection, and new vendor updates because patching alone does not remove the implant.

incidents.victim: Industrial control systems using Rockwell Automation / Allen-Bradley equipment
incidents.vector: Iran-linked actor scanned for and targeted Rockwell Automation FactoryTalk software and Allen-Bradley programmable logic controllers, expanding from earlier focus on Unitronics PLCs amid the US-Iran conflict.
incidents.impact: Roughly 5,600 IPs globally expose Rockwell SCADA/FactoryTalk services; CISA issued an advisory on April 7 mirroring researcher findings as the actor prepares for OT/ICS exploitation operations.

incidents.vector: Attackers breached an internal network storing customer data.
incidents.impact: Third reported hack of a major South African bank this year; scope of customer data exposure has not been disclosed publicly.

incidents.victim: 170+ Ukrainian prosecutors and militaries in Greece, Romania, Serbia
incidents.vector: GRU-linked operators broke into the email accounts of more than 170 Ukrainian prosecutors and allied military networks to steal investigative information.
incidents.impact: Mass compromise of Ukrainian prosecutorial offices plus militaries in three NATO/partner states; campaign forms part of a wider Russian intelligence-gathering effort flagged in March.

incidents.victim: Israeli water treatment & desalination operators (intended target)
incidents.vector: OT-targeted malware (named ZionSiphon) was designed to alter pump pressure and chlorine levels in Israeli water plants; it failed to trigger due to a bug in the IP-range check.
incidents.impact: Sabotage attempt failed in execution but demonstrates clear intent to attack Israeli water infrastructure with politically motivated strings invoking Iran, Palestine, and Yemen.

incidents.vector: Pro-Russian hacktivist group with links to Russian intelligence services attempted to intrude into a Swedish thermal power plant in western Sweden.
incidents.impact: Sweden's minister of civil defense disclosed the attempt this week; built-in plant safeguards detected and stopped the intrusion before any operational disruption.

incidents.vector: Attackers exfiltrated user data from McGraw Hill's Salesforce environment in a 2024 hacking wave, then leaked the records publicly after the company refused to pay a ransom.
incidents.impact: 13.5 million users' personal details — names, home addresses, emails, and phone numbers — leaked online.

incidents.vector: Attackers stole customer data from Hallmark's Salesforce environment during a 2024 hacking wave and leaked the records after Hallmark refused to pay a ransom.
incidents.impact: Personal information of 1.7 million Hallmark customers leaked publicly.

incidents.vector: Attackers compromised a Vercel developer with malware disguised as Roblox cheat software, then used that foothold to breach Vercel's environment and steal customer data.
incidents.impact: Customer data offered for sale on criminal forums; Vercel notified affected customers and recommended rotating keys and secrets; fallout has expanded to additional customers and third-party systems.

incidents.vector: Attackers exploited a vulnerability in the Kelp DAO restaking platform to withdraw funds as restaked Ether (rsETH) tokens.
incidents.impact: $292 million in crypto assets stolen — the largest crypto-heist of 2026 so far.

incidents.victim: LiteLLM, Checkmarx, Trivy users (and downstream organizations)
incidents.vector: Threat group used stolen GitHub developer credentials to push malicious packages (including a fake @bitwarden/cli) and to poison Checkmarx GitHub Actions workflows, harvesting cloud, AI API, and developer credentials at install time.
incidents.impact: Cascading supply-chain compromises across five package ecosystems; group threatens to release more than 300 GB of stolen data and is collaborating with extortion affiliates; security tools (Trivy, Checkmarx) themselves were the entry points.
